items.title: Trump criticizes UK and France, urges allies to secure their own Oil routes
items.summary: In a series of posts on Truth Social, US President Donald Trump criticized several US allies, including the United Kingdom (UK) and France, over their position following the recent US operation targeting Iran.

items.title: JPY: BoJ may advance hike on Iran conflict risk – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur says Japan’s inflation outlook remains contained, with core prices still within the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) 2% target range and second‑round effects not yet visible.

items.title: USD: Energy shock supports strength as Fed stays cautious – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman notes the US Dollar is trading at its strongest levels since May last year as the Middle East conflict fuels concerns over a prolonged energy price shock that could hit global growth outside the US harder.

items.title: RBA: May hike likely as inflation risks build – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team notes the RBA hiked in March citing persistent inflation, strong demand, a tight labour market and higher Oil prices.

items.title: Oil: War-driven volatility and structural risks – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank’s Global Strategist Michael Every highlights that Brent has eased to around $111 and WTI to $102 despite a Kuwaiti tanker being hit, while European and African Oil markets are tightening as Asia fills supply gaps. He cites Bloomberg’s warning of $140 Oil if Red Sea shipping is hit again.

items.title: NOK: Oil-driven gains seen reversing after war – Commerzbank
items.summary: Michael Pfister at Commerzbank notes that Norwegian Krone (NOK) has outperformed Swedish Krona (SEK) thanks to Norway’s energy-exporter status during the war-related commodity shock, despite similar rate expectations.

items.title: Euro area: Rising energy risks support ECB hike case – Nordea
items.summary: Nordea’s Chief Analyst Anders Svendsen and Chief Economist Tuuli Koivu note Euro area inflation rose to 2.5% year-on-year in March and is expected to approach 3% in coming months.

items.title: Australian Dollar rises as Trump’s peace call with Iran improves market mood
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades higher against its major currency peers, with the AUD/USD pair rising 0.15% to near 0.6865, during the European trading session on Tuesday.

items.title: BoJ: Data signal case for more tightening – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered analysts Nicholas Chia and Chong Hoon Park highlight that new Bank of Japan (BoJ) indicators show underlying inflation near or above the 2% target and a positive output gap since Q1 2022, suggesting growth above potential.

items.title: DXY: Softer tone as crude jumps and Fed stays steady – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chris Turner argues the Dollar (USD) could ease as US light crude trades above $100 and markets look for signs of de-escalation in the Middle East. A relaxed Federal Reserve (Fed) tone has pushed money markets back toward pricing a rate cut by year-end.

items.title: JPY: Intervention risk rises as BoJ seen hiking – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman highlights that the Japanese Yen (JPY) has held recent gains, with USD/JPY dipping below 160.00 as officials step up verbal intervention.

items.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Remains subdued near 0.5700, four-month lows
items.summary: NZD/USD extends its losing streak for the sixth successive day, trading around 0.5710 during the European hours on Tuesday. The technical analysis of the daily chart signals a persistent bearish bias as the pair is moving downwards within a descending channel pattern.
